rationale.R1.1: The draft's contribution claim (SOTA BLEU via two architectural tweaks) needs sharpening; the gap statement versus prior work is asserted but not motivated, and the thesis could be crisper.
rationale.R1.2: Several logical tensions exist: the 'state-of-the-art' claim is made before the comparison set is established; the claim of exceeding a Google production system is introduced late without grounding; training/eval set identity (WMT-2018 train → WMT-2018 test) needs consistency verification.
rationale.R1.3: The BLEU figures (28.7 vs 27.1) are central load-bearing claims with no pointer to a table or figure in this excerpt; the Vaswani et al. baseline figure cited may not match the commonly reported number (29.3 for the large model), creating a potential evidence-claim mismatch that must be resolved.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty claim rests on two modifications (context window size, pre-norm); the draft does not distinguish which modification drives the gain, nor does it situate these against contemporaneous work that already uses pre-norm or longer contexts.
rationale.R1.6: The prose is functional but has structural redundancies (training details scattered between body and appendix reference), passive-heavy sentences, and an abrupt closing sentence that weakens the contribution framing.
rationale.R1.7: The Vaswani et al. baseline BLEU figure cited (27.1) appears inconsistent with published results; the Google production system comparison lacks a citation; WMT-2018 and sacreBLEU references should be formally cited.
